name: Campfy
shortDescription: An app for buying all types of camping supplies, prioritizing a fast and convenient shopping experience.
overview: Camping is a popular outdoor activity that requires specialized gear and equipment. However, finding camping supplies can be time-consuming and frustrating, especially for beginners. Many existing platforms have complex navigation , overwhelming product selections , and lack personalized recommendations, making it difficult for users to find what they need quickly. This often leads to decision fatigue and a less enjoyable shopping experience.
refinedScope: Campfy is an e-commerce platform designed to streamline the process of purchasing camping supplies. It features a Suggestive flow with curated gear packages to reduce decision fatigue, a powerful product Compare tool for fast evaluation, and a simplified, three-step checkout. Campfy ensures users can quickly acquire necessary supplies through an intuitive and accessible interface.

children.title: Goal Statement
children.content: Our camping supply app will let users browse and discover essential gear through organized categories and utilize the compare feature to save time on research. This will specifically affect new campers who need to find essential gear for a trip. We will measure effectiveness by tracking first-time users when they sign up.

children.title: User Story
children.content: To better understand the needs and pain points of our target users, I created a user story that outlines the typical experience of someone looking to buy camping supplies. This user story helped me empathize with the users and identify key areas where our app can provide value. By focusing on the user's journey, I was able to design features that address their specific needs and enhance their overall shopping experience.

children.title: User Persona
children.content: Based on user research, I developed three user personas to represent the target audience for Campfy. A newbie camper who is new to camping and needs guidance on essential gear and equipment, an adventure mom on the go who wants to plan fun activities for her family, and an experienced camper who values convenience and efficiency when purchasing camping supplies.  These personas are looking for an app that can provide a streamlined shopping experience with personalized recommendations based on their camping needs.

children.title: Competitive Analysis
children.content: I conducted a competitive analysis of existing camping supply platforms to identify strengths, weaknesses, and opportunities for improvement. This analysis helped me understand the current market landscape and informed the design decisions for our app. By evaluating competitors, I was able to identify gaps in the market and develop features that differentiate our app from others.

content.text: I mapped out the user journey to visualize the steps users take when interacting with our app. This journey map helped me identify pain points and opportunities for improvement at each stage of the user's experience. By understanding the user journey, I was able to design features that enhance usability and provide a seamless shopping experience.

children.title: Brainstorming
children.content: During the brainstorming phase, I generated a wide range of ideas for features and functionalities that could enhance the user experience of our camping supply app. I focused on creating solutions that address the pain points identified in the discovery phase, such as simplifying slow navigation and complex checkout processes, while integrating guidance to combat the feeling of being overwhelmed by choices.

content.text: I created a site map to outline the structure and organization of our camping supply app. This site map shows the four main categories: Suggestive, Shop, User, and Cart. Each category contains subcategories that help users navigate the app and find the products they need quickly. The site map also highlights key features such as the Compare tool and streamlined checkout process, which are designed to enhance the user experience and make shopping for camping supplies more efficient.

I developed user flows to visualize the steps users take when interacting with our camping supply app. These flows helped me identify key touchpoints and interactions that are critical to the user experience. By mapping out the user flows, I was able to design features that enhance usability and provide a seamless shopping experience.

children.title: Paper Wireframe
children.content: I started the design phase by creating paper wireframes to quickly visualize the app's layout and features. I draw five quick-sketch versions for the home screen. Then I selected the best elements from each sketch to create a final paper wireframe. These wireframes helped me communicate my ideas with the team and gather feedback before moving on to digital designs.

children.title: Digital Wireframe
children.content: After creating paper wireframes, I developed digital wireframes to provide a more detailed representation of the app's design. The digital wireframes allowed us to refine the app's layout and features, making it easier to visualize the final product. This digital wireframe showcases the main user flow, from discovering personalized activities to planning the next adventure with friends.

children.title: Style Guide
children.content: The Campfy Style Guide establishes a visual identity based on natural, earthy tones (Dark Olive, Pale Green, Taupe) to evoke stability and comfort, reflecting the brand's goal of making camping preparation easy and stress-free. The logo prominently features two stylized mountains to anchor the brand in the outdoors, while the use of the Poppins and Afacad typefaces ensures both a clear brand presence and high readability for all product information.

children.title: Prototype
children.content: The transition from a mid-fidelity wireframe to a high-fidelity prototype is where a product finds its soul. For Campfy, my goal was to bridge the gap between "I want to go camping" and "I am ready to go." Through user research, I identified that beginners feel overwhelmed by gear specs and logistics. These four high-fidelity features were designed to replace that intimidation with confidence.

children.text: Problem: Complex multi-step checkouts are the #1 cause of cart abandonment in the travel/outdoor sector. Solution: By leveraging stored user profiles and digital wallet integration (Apple/Paypal), I narrowed the entire booking process down to three distinct clicks. Design Highlight: Optimized the UI for "thumb-zone" accessibility, placing the primary CTA buttons at the bottom of the screen to facilitate a smooth, one-handed checkout experience.

Problem: First-time campers don't know the difference between a 3-season tent and a bivy sack. Solution: I designed a dedicated "Starter" category that bundles essential gear into a single "Weekend Starter" package ($239). Design Highlight: Used high-quality lifestyle imagery and simplified "What's Inside" iconography to make the value proposition immediate and visual, reducing cognitive load.

Problem: Users often flip back and forth between product pages to compare weight, price, and ratings, leading to "tab fatigue." Solution: I created a "Compare" feature that allows users to select up to five products and view their specs side-by-side in a clean, tabular format. Design Highlight: Implemented a "sticky" header for the comparison table, ensuring the kit names remain visible even as the user scrolls through technical details.

Problem: A tent that works for a backyard campout might fail on a windy mountain trail. Solution: I integrated a Location-Aware Recommendation Engine. When a user inputs their destination (e.g., "Mount Rainier"), the app cross-references nearby trail data and suggests gear specifically rated for that terrain and elevation. Design Highlight: Added a "Recommended for your location" feature on Homepage that dynamically updates, creating a sense of personalized safety and expertise.
